description_long: This is a rustic barn structure with a steeply pitched gable roof made of wide, weathered wooden planks. The front features a row of tattered red fabric banners hanging from the eaves, partially obscuring the open entrance. The sides and rear are enclosed with a rough, vertical wooden plank fence, some of which are uneven and broken, giving the barn a dilapidated appearance. The roof extends well beyond the walls, providing extra shelter at the front. The upper gable ends have open slats and missing boards, allowing light and air to pass through. The overall construction suggests a utilitarian, aged agricultural building, likely used for storage or sheltering livestock.
description: A weathered wooden barn with a steep gable roof, tattered red banners at the front, and rough plank fencing enclosing the sides and rear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide gable roof, tattered red banners, and open entrance beneath the eaves.
description_view_1: Angled side view revealing the vertical plank fence, open gable slats, and the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full extent of the vertical plank fencing and the solid, unbroken roofline.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view highlighting the broken and uneven planks, as well as the open gable structure.
attribute_siding_pattern: vertical board siding with irregular gaps and broken planks
attribute_door_style: single large open entrance with no visible door, partially covered by hanging fabric banners
attribute_window_arrangement: open slats and missing boards in the upper gable ends, no formal windows
